title: Metaortheme type mu with paired meta-policy
description: R4: the selector is a TYPED OBJECT, not a bare string — a state-selecting procedure that cannot be inspected for kind and description is not a declared selector. Exclusivity of the declared state set is explicit; where selection is non-unique the record must say what a non-unique selection means. Exactly one of meta_policy or policy_ref must be present: a metaortheme without a paired meta-policy is not a metaortheme.

allOf.description: pairing rule: a declared meta-policy or an explicit reference to one

then.description: when the declared states are not mutually exclusive, selection may be non-unique; the record must declare what a non-unique selection means

version.description: ver(mu) — recorded per episode so audits can scope editions

members.description: S_mu, declared in advance; a set — duplicates are malformed

partition.description: states are exclusive+exhaustive ONLY if declared true; never assumed

exclusivity.description: declared exclusivity of S_mu. 'exclusive': at most one state obtains. 'overlapping': several may obtain together. 'uncertain': exclusivity itself is undetermined. The latter two require uncertainty_semantics.

uncertainty_semantics.description: what a non-unique or undetermined selection MEANS for conduct (required unless exclusivity is 'exclusive')

selector.description: select_mu — the state-selecting evidence procedure; partial (may return several states or undetermined). R4: a typed object, never a bare string.

meta_policy.description: pi_mu — conduct conditional on the obtaining state, incl. conduct on undetermined selection

policy_ref.description: reference to the paired meta-policy when it is recorded elsewhere
